items.title: Failure to rescue after TAVR: New study tracks outcomes at high- and low-volume hospitals
items.title_zh: STS/ACC TVT 注册研究:TAVR 术后“抢救失败”(FTR)率在高、低手术量医院间相近
items.summary_zh: 基于 STS/ACC TVT 注册研究对美国 808 家中心 2017–2023 年间 383,395 例 TAVR 手术的分析(中位年龄 79 岁),4,685 例(1.2%)患者发生重大并发症;发生重大并发症患者的院内死亡率高达 26.0%,而无并发症患者仅为 1.0%。整体“抢救失败”(failure-to-rescue, FTR)率约 11%,研究期间缓慢下降,且高手术量与低手术量医院的 FTR 率相近——提示既往观察到的“手术量—死亡率”负相关关系,主要源于高手术量中心并发症发生率更低,而非其抢救能力更强。研究发表于 JACC: Cardiovascular Interventions(2026;19[13]:1735-1744,第一作者 Tayyab Shah)。
items.summary_en: An STS/ACC TVT Registry analysis of 383,395 TAVR procedures across 808 US sites from 2017-2023 (median age 79) found major complications in 4,685 patients (1.2%), with in-hospital mortality of 26.0% among those with a major complication versus 1.0% among those without. The overall failure-to-rescue (FTR) rate was about 11%, declining slowly over the study period, and was similar at high- and low-volume hospitals — suggesting the well-known inverse volume-mortality relationship in TAVR is driven mainly by lower complication rates at high-volume centers rather than superior rescue capability once a complication occurs. Published in JACC: Cardiovascular Interventions (2026;19[13]:1735-1744; first author Tayyab Shah).
